mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicate robust demand outpacing supply constraints, forcing firms to raise prices and margins, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yields negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production signals strengthening economic activity and higher corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days specifically during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the predictive power vanishes when conditioning on non-recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing uncertainty and discount rates, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of high-inflation threshold crossings, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized labor market strength and residential investment expansion, boosting consumer confidence and corporate capex, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined positive momentum of PAYEMS and HOUST produces negative forward returns in >65% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment signals a synchronized economic contraction, reducing corporate profit margins and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during recessionary periods.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal of INDPRO and UNRATE yields positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample predictive accuracy.
